summary:
The Trading Pit scores 78/100, offering CFD, Futures, and Stocks challenges under one roof. Prime is the current active CFD model, with separate Futures Prime and Stocks products. All accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds, and all trading activity takes place in a simulated environment.

Payout schedules vary by product: Prime CFD pays every 14 days, Futures Prime pays every 14 days for the first two payouts then every 7 days after the second payout if profit is over $200, and Stocks requires 3 profitable trading days with at least $100 profit per day for the first payout. The 40% consistency rule applies during the challenge phase. Platform availability depends on challenge type, with Stocks using Volumetrica Web & Desktop. Traders can scale up to €5M.
pros: CFDs, Stocks, and Futures challenges available
Wide range of markets including 46 forex pairs
Multiple platform choices tailored by challenge type
10,000+ active monthly accounts and $14M+ in rewards
Blog, ebooks, webinars, podcasts, videos, and infographics for education
cons: Set minimum trading days across all challenges
High commission fees ($5 per lot forex, 0.20% crypto)
40% consistency rule limits single-day profits during challenge phase
Stocks profit share is 70%, lower than CFD and Futures
bestFor: Traders who want access to CFD, Futures, and Stocks markets under one firm, with structured challenges, multiple platform choices, and the ability to scale up to €5M
notIdealFor: Traders looking for a single simple payout structure across all products, or those who rely on third-party EAs or copy trading strategies

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On The Trading Pit
verdictBlocks.body:
The Trading Pit stands out with its CFD, Futures, and Stocks challenges, offering a unique multi-asset alternative to many prop firms. Traders can scale up to €5M, and payout schedules are tailored by product type. All accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds, and all trading takes place in a simulated environment.

Prime is the current active CFD model, offering an 80% profit split. Futures Prime requires 5 profitable trading days with at least $200 profit per day for the first two payouts, then allows payout requests every 7 days. Stocks first payout requires 3 profitable trading days with at least $100 per day. Platform availability depends on challenge type, with Stocks using Volumetrica Web & Desktop.

The 40% consistency rule applies during the challenge phase, and the news trading restriction is a 2-minute window around high-impact events. Trade coordination and copy trading are prohibited, and certain EA behaviour is banned. Overall, The Trading Pit is a solid choice for traders who want structured multi-asset challenges with transparent rules and strong educational resources.

faq.q: What broker does The Trading Pit use?
faq.a: The Trading Pit is not a broker-backed prop firm, but partners with different providers for liquidity and execution. For cTrader CFD trading, Tickmill is used as the liquidity provider. All accounts are demo accounts with virtual funds — all trading activity takes place in a simulated environment.

faq.q: Does The Trading Pit accept US clients?
faq.a: US and Canadian residents can access Futures Challenges only. CFD, Crypto, and Stock Challenges are not currently offered to US or Canadian traders.

faq.q: Is The Trading Pit legit or a scam?
faq.a: The Trading Pit is a legitimate prop firm, earning a 78/100 score from Best Prop Firms. It offers CFD, Futures, and Stocks challenges with the ability to scale up to €5M. The official site highlights 10,000+ active monthly accounts, $14M+ in rewards, 450K+ monthly trades, and coverage across 180+ countries.

faq.q: What is The Trading Pit's profit split?
faq.a: The Trading Pit's Prime challenges offer a fixed 80% profit split for CFD and Futures. Stocks challenges have a 70% profit share. Classic challenges are legacy and not the current main offering.

faq.q: Does The Trading Pit allow EAs and copy trading?
faq.a: Trade coordination and copy trading with other traders or accounts are prohibited. Certain EA behaviour is banned, and using third-party EAs can stop an account from passing or progressing. The rules restrict specific automated strategies rather than allowing EAs without limitation.
